item.title: Richard Caring sells majority stake in Ivy hospitality empire to Sheikh Tahnoon’s IHC
item.description: Deal includes an investment of more than £1bn

item.title: NFL’s media rights stand-off hits a plot twist
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: what Sunderland says about women’s football, cricket’s vote of confidence in the Gulf as a sports hub, and more

item.title: Superyacht maker Sanlorenzo rides personalisation wave
item.description: Italian group looks to sustain sales boom with hyper bespoke vessels including its latest built around a living tree

item.title: The damage wrought on the Middle East’s oil and gas supplies
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Qatar have both suffered significant damage to production capacity during the US-Israeli war against Iran

item.title: Jacob’s Creek should come in smaller bottles
item.description: Relaunch of popular 1990s tipple might need more than nostalgia to stand out from today’s crowd

item.title: Mortgage borrowers seek shorter-term deals as market volatility saps confidence
item.description: Flexibility is the watchword among UK borrowers amid signs of slowing activity in the housing market

item.title: BP’s proxy fight exposes bugs in shareholder voting machine
item.description: On contentious matters, investors are less likely to follow proxies

item.title: UK households turn to solar panels as energy bills bite
item.description: Fallout from Middle East crisis prompts more people to explore the alternative source of power

item.title: The UK police’s latest problem: crowds of unruly teens
item.description: Disorder in London and other cities threatens public sense of order, even though it may fall short of widespread criminality

item.title: Tofoo co-founder: ‘I’d lost £250,000 in investments before I started the company’
item.description: It was fourth time lucky for entrepreneur David Knibbs

item.title: Separating the signal from the noise in private credit
item.description: What matters most is how capital was deployed and underwritten

item.title: Anthropic closes in on OpenAI as US business use surges
item.description: Divergence reflects company’s recent rapid growth owing to strong interest in its Claude Code products

item.title: The next matcha: coffee chains bet on ube’s viral appeal
item.description: Costa and Starbucks launch purple drinks in effort to recreate viral success of matcha among young consumers

item.title: How Iran turned to memes to take on Trump
item.description: Regime accounts have saturated feeds with snarky online propaganda since US and Israel started bombing campaign

item.title: ‘Fortress China’ shows cracks as Iran war strains supply chains
item.description: Energy, chemicals and helium supply under pressure despite Beijing’s push to build up economic security

item.title: Clients’ barrage of AI-generated queries risks pushing up lawyers’ fees
item.description: Firms may raise prices for fixed-fee contracts if clients keep sending flurries of emails and letters

item.title: Cyber security stocks fall on worries over Anthropic’s advanced AI tool
item.description: Mythos model was able to detect critical software vulnerabilities that were missed by legacy systems

item.title: Molotov cocktail thrown at home of OpenAI chief Sam Altman
item.description: Suspect arrested outside AI start-up’s San Francisco headquarters after threatening to burn it down

item.title: Lloyds will not take legal action against UK’s £9bn car finance redress scheme
item.description: Britain’s largest provider of vehicle loans wants to draw a line under scandal that rocked the sector

item.title: Iran-linked ships dominate Hormuz traffic after ceasefire
item.description: At least nine of the 14 or so vessels to have transited chokepoint since the pause in fighting have ties to Tehran

item.title: Why has the UK economy been so badly hit by the Iran war?
item.description: Over-reliance on imported gas and worries over Starmer’s future likely to stall pick-up in momentum, analysts say

item.title: Crispin Odey abandons £79mn libel case against the FT over sexual misconduct claims
item.description: The FT had said 15 women were willing to testify in court against former hedge fund boss

item.title: Partners Group backs gating as private credit redemptions test liquidity
item.description: Swiss-listed private capital group seeks to ‘offer transparency’ with update against ‘complex’ market backdrop

item.title: Trump talks up Palantir days after latest short seller attack
item.description: US president’s endorsement follows criticism from famed investor Michael Burry

item.title: Ireland risks cutting off oil supply amid fuel protests, premier warns
item.description: Micheál Martin’s comments come as demonstrations threaten to spill over into Northern Ireland

item.title: Directors’ Deals: 3i boss backs US ambitions
item.description: Private equity group’s share price down by 40 per cent in the past six months

item.title: Middle East conflict drains Singapore’s marine fuel supplies
item.description: Growing shipping congestion around the world’s biggest refuelling port shrinks reserves

item.title: JPMorgan limbers up to become Olympic sponsor
item.description: Bank set to join select list of corporate partners ahead of 2028 Games in Los Angeles

item.title: Why I will always love Canary Wharf
item.description: The business district in London’s Docklands demonstrates what innovation and invention can achieve

item.title: Australia’s unquenchable thirst for diesel tested by Iran energy shock
item.description: Armada of tankers sailing from as far away as the US and UK as reliance on imports from Asia in focus during supply crunch
